channel.title: ttsc Blog
channel.description: Engineering notes, releases, and deep dives from ttsc.

item.title: Monorepos Need Project-Local Compiler Truth
item.description: ttsc resolves tsconfig, package plugins, config files, ttsx runtime layout, and VS Code server paths from the project that owns the file.

item.title: Boring Banners Matter For Published TypeScript Packages
item.description: @ttsc/banner adds package documentation headers to emitted JavaScript and declarations. It is small, but package polish is often small.

item.title: Strip Debug Code With A Compiler, Not A Regex
item.description: @ttsc/strip removes exactly configured statement-level calls and debugger statements during emit, without pretending to be a minifier.

item.title: Bundlers Should Not Bypass Your Compiler Plugins
item.description: @ttsc/unplugin brings the ttsc plugin pass into Vite, Webpack, Rollup, esbuild, Rspack, Next.js, Farm, and Bun.

item.title: The Browser Playground Should Run The Real Compiler
item.description: @ttsc/wasm lets docs, tutorials, and plugin demos run TypeScript-Go in a Web Worker with an in-memory project.

item.title: Your Editor Should Run The Project's Compiler
item.description: @ttsc/vscode resolves the workspace-installed ttsc instead of shipping its own compiler, so editor diagnostics match the build.

item.title: The Plugin Pitfalls I Already Hit For You
item.description: Most ttsc plugin failures are predictable: missing source, wrong package name, bad composition, stale Go env, and unsafe AST assumptions.

item.title: The Driver API Is The Plugin SDK
item.description: ttsc plugin authors should start from github.com/samchon/ttsc/packages/ttsc/driver, not raw TypeScript-Go internals.

item.title: Lint Rules Should Join The Engine, Not Fork The Project
item.description: @ttsc/lint contributor packages link Go rule sources into one lint binary, avoiding one process and one Program per rule pack.

item.title: CI Should Warm The Compiler, Not Fear It
item.description: ttsc prepare prebuilds plugin binaries so CI can separate cold plugin compilation from steady-state type-check and lint measurements.

item.title: One Flag Table To Rule The CLI
item.description: ttsc and ttsx share a generated flag schema so launcher parsing, docs, and native plugin allow-lists do not drift.

item.title: Typed Config Files Beat JSON Soup
item.description: ttsc plugins use dedicated config files such as lint.config.ts and strip.config.ts. The tsconfig plugin entry stays small and discoverable.

item.title: Benchmarks Are Boring Until They Save Your Architecture
item.description: The ttsc benchmark is not just a chart. It is how I keep compiler, lint, and format claims honest across real projects.

item.title: Replace tsc First, Then Replace The Toolchain
item.description: ttsc can start as a drop-in TypeScript-Go compiler path. The larger eslint/prettier/plugin migration can happen one layer at a time.

item.title: The Autofix Loop I Want For TypeScript
item.description: ttsc fix applies lint and format edits, then re-checks the project. It is the local cleanup command before CI runs ttsc --noEmit.

item.title: Format Should Be A Command, Not A Vibe
item.description: ttsc format is deliberately separate from ttsc --noEmit. Formatting writes source, lint gates source, and mixing the two makes the toolchain harder to trust.

item.title: Stop Shipping tsconfig Paths To Runtime
item.description: @ttsc/paths is the boring plugin that fixes a real build problem: TypeScript path aliases are compiler hints, not JavaScript runtime imports.

item.title: Writing TypeScript Plugins In Go Is Less Weird Than It Sounds
item.description: ttsc plugins use a JavaScript descriptor and a Go sidecar. That split looks unusual until you map it to TypeScript-Go's AST, Checker, and emit pipeline.

item.title: One TypeScript Plugin, Four Surfaces
item.description: A ttsc plugin is not just a CLI hook. The same compiler-powered package can run in ttsc, ttsx, bundlers, VS Code, and browser playgrounds.

item.title: Why ttsc Builds Plugins From Go Source
item.description: ttsc plugins are npm packages, but their compiler logic is Go source built once into a content-addressed cache. Here is why that tradeoff exists.

item.title: AI Code Needs A Compiler Harness, Not More CI Steps
item.description: In AI-heavy codebases, lint is no longer style police. It is the harness that keeps generated code mergeable, and it belongs in the compilation pass.

item.title: ttsx: The TypeScript Runner That Refuses To Run Broken Code
item.description: ttsx keeps the tsx-style workflow, but gates execution behind a real TypeScript-Go project check and the same ttsc plugin pipeline.

item.title: I Made TypeScript Plugins Install Like Normal npm Packages
item.description: typia and nestia no longer need fragile tsconfig plugin ceremony in ttsc. Install the package, keep the marker, and the host discovers it.

item.title: TypeScript Transformers Were Dying, So I Built ttsc
item.description: TypeScript-Go changes the ground under custom transformers. ttsc exists because typia, nestia, autobe, and the next compiler-powered tools need a real host.

item.title: 500x Faster TypeScript Lint: I Moved Lint Into TypeScript-Go
item.description: @ttsc/lint moves lint into the TypeScript-Go-backed check path and ships a separate native format command: 500x faster lint and 70x faster format on the VS Code fixture.
